mnemonics: Start from the End, Work Back, to Find Your Perfect Friend (the Nash Equilibrium).
Think like a Chess Master: Look at the final move first and reason backwards to your best first move.
The BI Cycle: End -> Decide -> Move Back -> Repeat until you find the NEat solution.
keyPoints: Solves finite sequential games by working from the terminal nodes back to the root.
The outcome is a Subgame Perfect Equilibrium (SPE), a refinement of Nash Equilibrium.
Assumes perfect information and common knowledge of rationality.
commonMistakes: Applying it to games with simultaneous moves or imperfect information.
Forgetting to consider all possible actions of other players at each step.
Assuming the process guarantees a unique solution; some games have multiple equilibria.
